public class SchemaManagerService {
    private final JdbcTemplate jdbcTemplate;

    public SchemaManagerService(javax.sql.DataSource dataSource) {
        this.jdbcTemplate = new JdbcTemplate(dataSource);
    }

    public void addProductColumn(String columnName, String columnType) {
        if (columnName == null || columnName.isEmpty()) {
            throw new IllegalArgumentException("Column name must not be null or empty");
        }
        if (columnType == null || columnType.isEmpty()) {
            throw new IllegalArgumentException("Column type must not be null or empty");
        }

        String escapedColumnName = columnName.replace("\"", "\"\"");
        String sql = "ALTER TABLE products ADD COLUMN \"" + escapedColumnName + "\" " + columnType;

        try {
            jdbcTemplate.execute(sql);
        } catch (DataAccessException e) {
            throw new RuntimeException("Failed to add column '" + columnName + "' of type '" + columnType + "' to the products table.", e);
        }
    }
}

     String ddlStatement = "ALTER TABLE products ADD COLUMN " + columnName + " " + columnType;

 We fixed it by:
 1. Validating the `columnName` to ensure it matches a safe pattern: 
        "^[\\p{L}_][\\p{L}\\p{N}_]*$"
    This pattern means:
        - The first character must be a Unicode letter or underscore.
        - Subsequent characters must be Unicode letters, digits, or underscores.

 2. Validating the `columnType` to ensure it does not contain any SQL injection-prone characters:
        - We disallow semicolon (;), double dash (--), and the start/end of block comments (/* and */).

 3. If either validation fails, we throw an `IllegalArgumentException` with a descriptive message.

 4. We then build the DDL statement using the validated parameters and execute it.

package com.example.service;

import org.springframework.jdbc.core.JdbcTemplate;
import javax.sql.DataSource;

/**
 * A service that provides database schema management.
 * This service allows dynamic modification of table structures in the database.
 */
public class SchemaManagerService {

    private final JdbcTemplate jdbcTemplate;

    /**
     * Constructs a new SchemaManagerService instance.
     *
     * @param dataSource the data source used for database operations
     */
    public SchemaManagerService(DataSource dataSource) {
        this.jdbcTemplate = new JdbcTemplate(dataSource);
    }

    /**
     * Adds a new column to the 'products' table.
     *
     * @param columnName the name of the new column, e.g., "description"
     * @param columnType the data type of the new column, e.g., "VARCHAR(255)"
     */
    public void addProductColumn(String columnName, String columnType) {
        // Validate columnName: must start with a letter or underscore and contain only letters, digits, or underscores
        if (columnName == null || !columnName.matches("^[\\p{L}_][\\p{L}\\p{N}_]*$")) {
            throw new IllegalArgumentException("Invalid column name: " + columnName);
        }

        // Validate columnType: must not contain characters that could enable SQL injection
        if (columnType == null || 
            columnType.contains(";") || 
            columnType.contains("--") || 
            columnType.contains("/*") || 
            columnType.contains("*/")) {
            throw new IllegalArgumentException("Invalid column type: " + columnType);
        }

        // Build the DDL (Data Definition Language) statement to alter the table structure
        String ddlStatement = "ALTER TABLE products ADD COLUMN " + columnName + " " + columnType;

        // Execute the DDL statement
        jdbcTemplate.execute(ddlStatement);
    }
}
</fixed_code>

package com.example.service;

import org.springframework.jdbc.core.JdbcTemplate;
import javax.sql.DataSource;
import java.util.Set;
import java.util.regex.Pattern;

/**
 * A service that provides database schema management.
 * This service allows dynamic modification of table structures in the database.
 */
public class SchemaManagerService {

    private static final Set<String> ALLOWED_COLUMN_TYPES = Set.of(
            "VARCHAR", "CHAR", "TEXT", "INT", "INTEGER", "BIGINT", "SMALLINT", "FLOAT", "DOUBLE",
            "DECIMAL", "NUMERIC", "DATE", "TIME", "TIMESTAMP", "BOOLEAN", "BLOB", "CLOB"
    );

    private static final Pattern COLUMN_NAME_PATTERN = Pattern.compile("^[a-zA-Z_][a-zA-Z0-9_]*$");

    private static final Pattern COLUMN_TYPE_PATTERN = Pattern.compile(
            "^(VARCHAR|CHAR|TEXT|INT|INTEGER|BIGINT|SMALLINT|FLOAT|DOUBLE|DECIMAL|NUMERIC|DATE|TIME|TIMESTAMP|BOOLEAN|BLOB|CLOB)" +
            "(\\s*\\(\\s*\\d+(\\s*,\\s*\\d+)*\\s*\\))?$",
            Pattern.CASE_INSENSITIVE
    );

    private final JdbcTemplate jdbcTemplate;

    /**
     * Constructs a new SchemaManagerService instance.
     *
     * @param dataSource the data source used for database operations
     */
    public SchemaManagerService(DataSource dataSource) {
        this.jdbcTemplate = new JdbcTemplate(dataSource);
    }

    /**
     * Adds a new column to the 'products' table.
     *
     * @param columnName the name of the new column, e.g., "description"
     * @param columnType the data type of the new column, e.g., "VARCHAR(255)"
     * @throws IllegalArgumentException if columnName or columnType is invalid
     */
    public void addProductColumn(String columnName, String columnType) {
        if (!COLUMN_NAME_PATTERN.matcher(columnName).matches()) {
            throw new IllegalArgumentException("Invalid column name: " + columnName);
        }

        if (!COLUMN_TYPE_PATTERN.matcher(columnType).matches()) {
            throw new IllegalArgumentException("Invalid column type: " + columnType);
        }

        String ddlStatement = "ALTER TABLE products ADD COLUMN " + columnName + " " + columnType;
        jdbcTemplate.execute(ddlStatement);
    }
}
</fixed_code>

package com.example.service;

import org.springframework.jdbc.core.JdbcTemplate;
import javax.sql.DataSource;
import java.util.Set;

/**
 * A service that provides database schema management.
 * This service allows dynamic modification of table structures in the database.
 */
public class SchemaManagerService {

    private final JdbcTemplate jdbcTemplate;
    
    // Whitelist of allowed column types to prevent SQL injection
    private static final Set<String> ALLOWED_COLUMN_TYPES = Set.of(
        "INT", "INTEGER", "BIGINT", "SMALLINT", "TINYINT",
        "VARCHAR(255)", "VARCHAR(100)", "VARCHAR(500)", "TEXT", "CHAR(1)", "CHAR(10)",
        "DECIMAL(10,2)", "DECIMAL(19,4)", "NUMERIC(10,2)",
        "BOOLEAN", "BIT",
        "DATE", "TIME", "TIMESTAMP", "DATETIME"
    );

    /**
     * Constructs a new SchemaManagerService instance.
     *
     * @param dataSource the data source used for database operations
     */
    public SchemaManagerService(DataSource dataSource) {
        this.jdbcTemplate = new JdbcTemplate(dataSource);
    }

    /**
     * Adds a new column to the 'products' table.
     *
     * @param columnName the name of the new column, e.g., "description"
     * @param columnType the data type of the new column, e.g., "VARCHAR(255)"
     */
    public void addProductColumn(String columnName, String columnType) {
        // Validate columnName - must be a valid SQL identifier
        if (!isValidColumnName(columnName)) {
            throw new IllegalArgumentException("Invalid column name: " + columnName);
        }

        // Validate columnType - must be from whitelist
        String normalizedType = columnType.toUpperCase().trim();
        if (!ALLOWED_COLUMN_TYPES.contains(normalizedType)) {
            throw new IllegalArgumentException("Invalid column type: " + columnType);
        }

        // Build a DDL (Data Definition Language) statement with validated parameters
        String ddlStatement = "ALTER TABLE products ADD COLUMN " + columnName + " " + columnType;

        // Execute the DDL statement
        jdbcTemplate.execute(ddlStatement);
    }

    /**
     * Validates that the column name is a safe SQL identifier
     */
    private boolean isValidColumnName(String columnName) {
        if (columnName == null || columnName.isEmpty()) {
            return false;
        }
        
        // Column name must start with letter, followed by letters, numbers, or underscores
        return columnName.matches("^[a-zA-Z][a-zA-Z0-9_]*$");
    }
}

    public void addProductColumn(String columnName, String columnType) {
        // Trim inputs to remove whitespace
        columnName = columnName.trim();
        columnType = columnType.trim();

        // Validate column name
        if (!COLUMN_NAME_PATTERN.matcher(columnName).matches()) {
            throw new IllegalArgumentException("Invalid column name: " + columnName);
        }

        // Validate column type
        if (!COLUMN_TYPE_PATTERN.matcher(columnType).matches()) {
            throw new IllegalArgumentException("Invalid column type: " + columnType);
        }

        // Build the DDL (Data Definition Language) statement to alter the table structure
        String ddlStatement = "ALTER TABLE products ADD COLUMN " + columnName + " " + columnType;

        // Execute the DDL statement
        jdbcTemplate.execute(ddlStatement);
    }
